title: calanthe woodfordii
intro: Calanthe woodfordii, also known as the orchid calanthe, is a rare and endangered species of plant native to New Zealand. This plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties attributed to its ability to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Calanthe woodfordii are believed to include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a valuable remedy for wound healing and immune system support. Calanthe woodfordii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and poultices, although its use is strictly regulated due to its endangered status and potential toxicity if not properly handled.
